summary: Address all four Greptile findings from PR #816: serialize OAuth refresh under a sync.Mutex with stale-token double-check, tighten cache file permissions to 0o600/0o700, gate the Windows-only PowerShell token-persist on runtime.GOOS, and add WITH to the read-only SQL validator's banned-token regex.
reason: PR #816 surfaced one P0 (concurrent RefreshAccessToken can race the rotation chain — directly violates the standing refresh-token persistence constraint), two P1 hardening items (cache files were 0o644 world-readable; PowerShell exec ran unconditionally on non-Windows hosts and produced noisy stderr), and a defense-in-depth item on the SQL allowlist (CTE-wrapped writes like `WITH x AS (...) DELETE FROM t` were caught by SQLite read-only mode but bypassed the validator regex). All four must land before resubmission so the Greptile thread is fully resolved.
validated_outcome: go build ./... clean; refreshIfStale double-checks Config.AuthHeader() under the lock so a second concurrent 401 reuses the rotated token instead of replaying the invalidated one; cache writes now use 0o700/0o600; PowerShell persist is no-op outside windows; SQL validator rejects WITH in addition to the prior DML/DDL/PRAGMA set.
